title: stillingia discolor
intro: Stillingia discolor, also known as Queen's Root or Indian Tallow, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant's roots and rhizomes contain stillingine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Stillingia discolor include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Stillingia discolor include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
